name: MCMC Auto - Arlington
description_short: MCMC Auto is a family-owned used car dealership offering in-house and external financing to buyers with all credit types, including Buy Here Pay Here (BHPH) options.
description_long:
MCMC Auto – Arlington has been family-owned and operated since 1969, now in its third generation of family management. Located at 7401 S Cooper St in Arlington, Texas, the dealership has served over 250,000 North Texas residents and reports that over 80,000 customers have purchased multiple vehicles from them. Manager Mark Cochrane leads the Arlington location.

MCMC Auto specializes in financing used vehicle purchases for consumers with less-than-ideal credit. They offer both internal and external lending programs designed to work with borrowers regardless of credit situation. Their key service is Buy Here Pay Here (BHPH) financing, which allows customers to finance vehicles directly through the dealership. All vehicles receive a multi-point inspection by certified technicians and come with a 4-month/4,000-mile warranty on most vehicles. Extended service contracts are available at $0 down. The dealership also reports customer payment history to Experian monthly, helping customers rebuild credit through positive payment reporting.

What distinguishes MCMC Auto is their commitment to relationship-based lending without predatory practices. They explicitly state they do not use starter interrupt devices or beeping payment reminder systems, positioning trust as central to their business model. They offer a "MyCar Program" that provides discounted prices and incentives on future purchases when customers reach low balances. Their large lot inventory and multi-location presence (implied by the location-specific URL) provide selection and accessibility.

However, as a used car dealership offering BHPH financing rather than a traditional personal lender, MCMC Auto's primary product is vehicle financing secured by the car itself, not unsecured personal loans. While they facilitate credit building through Experian reporting, their core business model depends on vehicle collateral and the dealership-customer relationship. Consumers seeking unsecured personal loans should explore alternatives, though MCMC Auto's financing options work well for those specifically needing vehicle purchases with flexible credit approval.
pros: Reports payment history to Experian monthly to help customers rebuild credit
Offers financing to all credit types, including those with less-than-perfect credit
Internal and external lending programs available to increase approval odds
4-month/4,000-mile warranty on most vehicles and multi-point inspection by certified technicians
Extended service contracts available at $0 down payment
No starter interrupt devices or payment beeping systems—relies on trust-based relationship
MyCar Program provides discounted prices and incentives on future purchases when balance is low
cons: Primary product is vehicle financing secured by collateral, not unsecured personal loans
Buy Here Pay Here financing typically carries higher interest rates and payment frequency requirements than traditional auto loans
Website pre-approval form shows 'Server error' message, indicating potential technical issues with online application process
Limited information about specific APR ranges, loan terms, or pricing transparency on website
Dealership model means financing is conditional on purchasing a vehicle from their inventory
best_for: Consumers with poor or rebuilding credit seeking to purchase a used vehicle
Buyers who need vehicle financing and want to simultaneously rebuild credit history
Customers looking for a dealership with transparent, trust-based lending practices without device-based payment enforcement
North Texas residents seeking Buy Here Pay Here financing options with local relationship-based service
diagnosis: MCMC Auto is best for North Texas consumers with fair or poor credit who specifically need to purchase a used vehicle and want to rebuild credit simultaneously through positive payment reporting. The main caveat is that this is a dealership-based BHPH lender, not a traditional personal loan provider—financing is vehicle-dependent, and BHPH loans typically carry higher rates and require more frequent payments than standard auto loans.
services: Buy Here Pay Here (BHPH) vehicle financing
In-house lending programs for customers with all credit types
Used vehicle sales with multi-point inspection
Monthly credit reporting to Experian for payment history
MyCar Program with discounted prices and future purchase incentives
